item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this one, really nice touches.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded: Posting here to note that the crosslink proposed above has been made, as I am unable to edit the original comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Once someone finished reading this well written article, they became a member of the Children of God.
Upvoted :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dislike this skip a great deal. I know it's old, and maybe that's the cause of my distaste. It hasn't aged well at all, it reeks of Series I with all the clichés and fallen-out-of-favor styles that go with that description. It's overly expositive, uninteresting, unimaginative and flat out boring. It lacks a hook and seems extremely similar to a plethora of other, better written skips.

Overall, this is a hard downvote without the shadow of a doubt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it any coincidence that this slot is right next to SCP-2439?

In all seriousness, this is great. Loved every second of it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, isn't this the same SCP as the one in the slot immediately before this one?
It works if it is, and it makes it slightly more horrifying and/or sad I guess.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Other possible links, mostly for procedure development I guess:
SCP-3004 has a very similar faith mechanism and also has a letter from a "Child of God," although I don't know if the link is explicit.

SCP-3002 has a similar spread mechanism. SCP-2950 has a similar threat type but opposite containment. SCP-3125 is similar in growth and containment.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Proposed Edits
- Proposed Edits

The crosslinks proposed are to link SCP-2440 to the GoIs that it mentions, specifically, the Global Occult Coalition, the Serpent's Hand, and the Chaos Insurgency.

Editing this:

In addition, it is believed that SCP-2440 is aware of other organizations such as the Global Occult Coalition, the Serpent’s Hand, and the Chaos Insurgency.

To this:

In addition, it is believed that SCP-2440 is aware of other organizations such as the Global Occult Coalition, the Serpent’s Hand, and the Chaos Insurgency.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If infection can be spread so easily and there's a hostile group still at large dedicated to spreading it, I find it hard to believe that the Foundation would be able to have kept this thing from taking over the world by now; kind of breaks my suspension of disbelief.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All interviews have concluded that SCP-2440 is inimical to human life

What about the interviews leads to that conclusion? I would assume that it said "everyone but my cultists are false believers", meaning it would kill all but a handful of humanity of it got loose, but if you aren't going to convey what it said in the interviews, you might as well leave out the mention of interviews and say something like "based on analysis of captured scripture, it is concluded that SCP-2440 is inimical to human life".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could the sealed king be the foolish king from the Church of the Broken God? Then Ma'tol could be the broken god itself. While the lores aren't exact, they're similar enough that I was actually a little surprised not to find that particular GOI tag at the bottom of the page.

item.title: Re: He who loves us the most.
item.content:encoded: You have to remember that the scripture is written by a cult of fanatics devoted to Xiolt-la, and should not be considered an unbiased or uninfluenced group of people. Half of the stuff they're preaching is straight from Xiolt-la's own mouth.

item.title: Re: He who loves us the most.
item.content:encoded: Thaumiel SCPs are used to contain other SCPs. I don't think this fits the bill. Frankly, I don't see how this is at all different from the legions of sealed/banished/forgotten gods we've already got, and have downvoted accordingly.

item.title: He who loves us the most.
item.content:encoded:
"He did not seek to twist our minds to serve for him. He only gave us the tools and power that we needed to live in peace and prosperity. He created great tools and gifted them to us, his true people."

"Praise the great god, Xiolt-la, he who brings fire and death upon his enemies. May he purge the world of the false believers."

"The entity is capable of controlling the actions of multiple infected individuals to some degree, but can extend its influence past infected individuals if it reaches a certain degree of awareness in the world."

"The old gods sought to destroy him for this. They could not comprehend a god such as him. In their twisted hearts, they believed that no god cared for his creations. Xiolt-la, lover of all, did."

-If he did not seek to twist our minds, why would he control people? Why would he need to infect so many people to have an influence in the world? Even though these documents were captured during an assault on a Children of God compound, which he apperad to have control of. An appearent god, being or entity might have done these things other than our extra-dimensional entity. Xiolt-la in the story might be diffrent than the subject.And the Children of God might have a false belief in the one who they praise.As Xiolt-la being described in the documents, he would not appear to be the one who controlls these people.Also Xiolt-la was never called "King" or "Prince". He was only called "Lord".However, Prince of Chaos, Ma'tol, was also "banished" and seems to act more likely to the descriptions of this entity.

Due to the compulsive and mind-affective effects of the entity and the posibility of it infecting multiple individuals, also to prevent this,

"SCP-2440’s existence is to be kept from all individuals lacking at least 4/2440 clearance. Any uncleared individual who learns of SCP-2440’s existence is to be issued several rounds of Class-X amnestics, until researchers agree by a unanimous vote that no more amnestics are required."

I would like to suggest a change of its object class from "Keter" to "Thaumiel".

item.title: hold on...
item.content:encoded: Doesn't this very article spread knowledge of the entity? This seems pretty obvious to me…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
According to the Tag Guide: meta - SCP affects or is affected by its documentation, or requires containment procedures regarding the way it is documented.

Also,

A meta object is an object that affects its documentation, is affected by its documentation, or has containment procedures that extend to its documentation. This sometimes overlaps with infohazard, but is distinct. SCP-048 is a prime example of this; documenting an object as SCP-048 causes accidents to happen, but speaking about SCP-048 or explaining it is harmless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could someone explain why this article has the "meta" tag, please? I feel like I'm missing something big here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is my feeling exactly. It's very well written and the ideas aren't bad, but there's nothing exciting or original either. Neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't we already have something that gets stronger the more people know of it? And I know we have tons of barely-contained Old Gods… This one doesn't do anything for me, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exactly what went through my head when I read it. It also makes me think of that thing down in Samocrathe, what with the article itself almost infecting individuals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This sounds heavy. The idea that acknowledging its existence is what gives it power appeals to me, because I was wondering about an idea like that for some time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure how I feel about another old god. It doesn't get a downvote from me just because it's not poorly written and it seems like it may have potential. But it's gonna need to develop into something more interesting or unique before I can give it an upvote.

item.title: Re: Author Overview
item.content:encoded: A bit of both. It's not critical for understanding the article, but at the same time, it gives you an idea of the history that the Foundation has had with these guys.

item.title: Re: Author Overview
item.content:encoded: Ah, okay. Was that something that we should have gotten from the article, or is it a nice easter egg for those who know their firebombings?

item.title: Re: Author Overview
item.content:encoded: The firebombing of Dresden was used to destroy the first return of Xiolt-la, after the Foundation hastily called in an air-strike when they realized that the situation was getting out of hand, and that they were dealing with an entity much more powerful than any of them realized.

item.title: Re: Author Overview
item.content:encoded:
Scan yourself an F-
JK <3"

The firebombing of Dresden in WWII, an event lambasted by many people for seeming completely out of line, and having no relevance to bombing actual wartime targets. It was (somewhat shoddily) explained away in a 1953 air force report, because nobody talked about it before then.

item.title: Re: Author Overview
item.content:encoded: uh, there are exactly two famous firebombings that come to mind, Tokyo and Dresden. I have no idea what either of them has to do with this.

item.title: Re: Author Overview
item.content:encoded: I'm either not thinking laterally enough or I'm not well-versed in history enough to get the reference. Take pity on me? ;A;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. Any other thoughts on the idea/execution itself?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Darn, this kinda nullifies an idea I had once't. Oh well. A little overbearing in the ancient lore stuff, but overall written well enough for a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: By bloated I meant the doubled words and superfluous stuff you removed. Much improved.

item.title: Dayum
item.content:encoded: This one hit heavy on the factions. Hope more stuff like this comes (tales, related skips etc.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, okay. That'll show me to post before reading all the way to the end. >_>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, they found that out through the incident that's mentioned in the last addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, wait, the Foundation deliberately infected 10,000 people with this thing to see what happens?

item.title: Re: Author Overview
item.content:encoded: It's not a reference to the article that I think that you're thinking of. It's a reference to an actual historical event.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Made some severe edits thanks to this. I'm not entirely sure of what you mean by bloated writing though, so I couldn't do much with that.

Changed all of the designations though, and the other problems mentioned here.

item.title: Re: Author Overview
item.content:encoded: I think I get the reference… And honestly? It hurts the skip over-all to be tied to such a(n) (in)famous article. I almost no-voted on that provocation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just FYI, the Children of God was the name of a real cult, who were creepy in a non-anomalous but seriously disturbing way.

item.title: Re: Author Overview
item.content:encoded: …Free Church of the Universal Kingdom plants?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
..

CASE NIGHTMARE GREEN is apparently on. Clef might be our Angleton but we don't have a SCORPION STARE equivalent. Nor a Bob. Fuck. Where's my violin..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not that I'm necessarily against the creation of pantheons/GOIs, but his one seems a little forced. It may be best to cut down this article and create a companion tale for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-2440 infects individuals with the knowledge of an entity designated as SCP-2440-1. SCP-2440-1 is an extra-dimensional being that is capable of exerting influence in this reality through individuals infected with SCP-2440, hereby designated as SCP-2440-2. The entity is capable of controlling the actions of infected individuals to some degree, but can extend its influence past infected individuals if it reaches a certain degree of awareness."

This paragraph is everything I hate about naming conventions. Don't give us a three part SCP with identical sounding names when each part is so completely different. There is no reason to give the infected their own designation. There is no reason to give the memetic agent its own designation either. "SCP-2440 is a parasitic mental entity whose strength grows through the spread of a memetic agent through a population." Boom. Now at the end of the one sentence I know what all three parts of the SCP are and I only have one designation to keep track of.

Plus, "In brief occasions when SCP-2440 infection exceeded 201 individuals, Foundation personnel were able to communicate with SCP-2440-1 briefly." —briefly twice in one sentence?

"An organization of individuals, known as the “Children of God,” composed of SCP-2440-2 individuals, is currently known to operate under SCP-2440-1. " —individuals twice in one sentence? (not to mention, what the hell else would an organization be composed of but individuals)

Clean up your language; this reads like it's trying too hard to sound "Technical" and comes off as sloppy and bloated.

edit: Plus dude look at your containment procedures: "Infected individuals are to be mind-wiped, then reminded of what they were made to forget and then re-wiped several times until the committee of science says to stop." I don't think a dedicated MTF makes much sense for this SCP either. A SCP that is likely to require regular armed intervention and specialized tools merits an MTF- this does neither.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Less than 200 individuals infected: SCP-2440-2 instances demonstrate a heightened interest in literature, images, and movies of the horror, fantasy, or science fiction genre. Said individuals often express interest in writing or sharing these works with other individuals. This is believed to increase the infection rate of SCP-2440.

Me senses a reference to this site and its members.

item.title: Author Overview
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to all of the assorted members of chat who looked at this.

This was written out of a random thought that I had while in the middle of class today. Who says school doesn't teach you anything?

Also, bonus points if you get the reference in the last addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Praise be to Xiolt-la. If you're here, you're already part of Him.
